name: White-rumped Vulture
conservationEfforts: Ban on veterinary diclofenac in India, Nepal, Pakistan, and Bangladesh. Vulture safe zones established. Captive breeding program by BNHS and RSPB has produced over 700 birds.
description: Once the most common large raptor in the world, the white-rumped vulture suffered a 99.9% population crash in South Asia due to the veterinary drug diclofenac, which is lethal to vultures.
